dogs.name: Koda
dogs.notes: Senior dog — MAX 5 miles per day at relaxed pace

dogs.name: Monty
dogs.notes: Young and energetic — use beaches and off-lead fields to burn energy

stays.name: Home in Gretna — Glamping Pod (Hosted by Gareth)
stays.access: Key safe to the right of the main pod door — code 0504

bike_hire.name: Road bike hire near Gretna
bike_hire.note: Quiet B7076 runs parallel to the M74 — gentle lanes for a short morning spin before a long drive day
bike_hire.climb_note: Flat area — best cycling is south towards Carlisle or north into Dumfries. Look for Moffat Water valley climbs if heading north.

stays.name: Rowardennan Lodge 10 (Airbnb)
stays.location: Rowardennan Hotel, Rowardennan, Loch Lomond, G63 0AR
stays.access: Key safe left of door, above black letterbox — code 1952. Barrier into lodge site: 1424. Take first left after barrier, Lodge 10 is immediately left after the little bridge. Park in space to left of lodge.
stays.directions: Follow signs for Drymen then Balmaha. Through Balmaha village past The Oak Tree (on left), then steep uphill right. Long twisty loch-side road (~15 min, loch on left) — large potholes, take care. Pass Rowardennan lodges on left, then Rowardennan Hotel. Into hotel car park, head left to barrier (code 1424), take first left.
stays.hot_water: Cupboard behind main bedroom door — boiler inside. Flick switch to 'timed' (heats overnight). Use 'booster' dial for immediate hot water.

bike_hire.name: Lomond Adventure, Balloch (14 miles south)
bike_hire.note: The B837 Drymen–Rowardennan lochside road is nearly traffic-free. The 26-mile Lomond Loop via Drymen is a classic. Bring road bike from home if possible — hire options are limited in Rowardennan itself.
bike_hire.climb_note: Rest and Be Thankful (A83, Cairndow) is 20 miles west — a classic Scottish climb, 5.7km at 7%. Also: the Crow Road from Lennoxtown and the Duke's Pass near Aberfoyle.

bike_hire.name: Nevis Cycles, Fort William (8 miles)
bike_hire.note: A82 through Glencoe is a legendary road climb (steep sides, dramatic scenery). Glen Nevis road from Fort William is quieter and excellent for road bikes.
bike_hire.climb_note: Glencoe Pass (A82) is right on your doorstep — 6km at 5% with dramatic scenery. Glen Nevis from Fort William is quieter. Bealach na Ba near Applecross (60 miles north) is Scotland's hardest road climb.

stays.name: Glendale Airbnb
stays.location: Lower Milovaig, Glendale, Isle of Skye, IV55 8WS

bike_hire.name: Island Cycles, Portree (25 miles)
bike_hire.note: The Duirinish peninsula from Glendale is almost completely traffic-free — ideal road cycling. Neist Point and back is 12 miles with sea views. The Trotternish ridge road (Portree–Uig–Staffin loop ~45 miles) is Skye's classic road ride.
bike_hire.climb_note: Duirinish peninsula from Glendale has near-zero traffic. The Quiraing road (northern Skye) is a famous photogenic climb. Bealach na Ba (Applecross, mainland) is a half-day ride from the ferry.

bike_hire.name: Bothy Bikes, Drumnadrochit (5 miles)
bike_hire.note: The Loch Ness 360 Trail runs the full south shore — quieter and hillier than the A82. General Wade's Military Road on the south side is a superb quiet ride with loch views.
bike_hire.climb_note: General Wade's Military Road on the south shore of Loch Ness is quiet and lumpy. The climb from Fort Augustus to the Corrieyairack Pass is legendary but gravel. Drumnadrochit to Cannich is good tarmac.

bike_hire.name: Biketrax, Edinburgh (4 miles from Portobello)
bike_hire.note: From Portobello: coastal promenade west to Leith → Cramond → Dalmeny = 30 miles flat. Arthur's Seat loop is 6 miles. Pentland Hills are 10 miles south for proper climbing.
bike_hire.climb_note: Arthur's Seat (Holyrood Park road) is 2km at 9% from the gates — start from Portobello, 4 miles. Pentland Hills (Flotterstone) has multiple climbs 10 miles south. Lammermuir Hills are 20 miles east.

bike_hire.name: Cycle Heaven, York city centre
bike_hire.note: York → Stamford Bridge → Pocklington loop is 38 miles on quiet lanes. Sutton Bank (North Yorkshire Moors, 35 miles north) is an iconic 1-in-4 climb — epic day out.
bike_hire.climb_note: Sutton Bank is 35 miles north — 2km at 13%, famous Tour de Yorkshire finish. Rouleur 100 route passes Helmsley. Cold Kirby and Kilburn Bank are gentler alternatives nearby.

days.title: Pulloxhill → Gretna Green
map_waypoints: Leicester Forest East Services M1 J21
Tebay Services Westmorland M6 J38 northbound Cumbria
Gretna Green Famous Blacksmiths Shop DG16

stops.name: Co-op Food — Gretna
stops.detail: In Gretna town, ~0.25 mile from the Blacksmiths Shop. Good for stocking up on arrival. Open every day 07:00–22:00.
stops.maps_query: Co-op Food Annan Road Gretna DG16 5DH

stops.name: Leicester Forest East Services
stops.detail: ~100 miles from Pulloxhill. Grass area for dogs. Coffee stop. Aim ~10:45.
stops.maps_query: Leicester Forest East Services M1 LE3 3GB

stops.name: Tebay Services (M6 J38)
stops.detail: Best motorway services in UK. Farm shop, outdoor seating, dog walk field. Aim ~13:00. Fuel here is expensive — Penrith Tesco (J40, 5 min off motorway) is significantly cheaper if the tank is low. Dogs: welcome in foyer areas only — not permitted in the Kitchen dining room. Outdoor options: woodland picnic area and duck pond seating (both traffic-free, dog-friendly). Dog walk along Howgill Fells path (keep on lead, poo bins provided). Adjacent Westmorland Hotel (same site) serves dinner 18:00–21:00 at the Bretherdale restaurant — pet-friendly rooms available if ever needed (dog bowls, Lily’s Kitchen treats, cork floors; book direct on 015396 24351).

stops.name: Gretna Green Famous Blacksmiths Shop
stops.detail: Walk the dog-friendly grounds. Indoor museum not dog-friendly.
stops.maps_query: Gretna Green Famous Blacksmiths Shop DG16

eating.name: Tebay Services Farm Kitchen
eating.detail: Dogs in foyer areas only — not in the Kitchen dining room. Eat at the outdoor duck pond or woodland picnic tables with the dogs, or one person orders while the other stays in the foyer with them.

eating.name: Smiths at Gretna Green
eating.detail: Dog-friendly restaurant on site — book a table.
eating.maps_query: Smiths at Gretna Green Restaurant DG16 5EA

notes: Fill tank before leaving — Tesco Flitwick (5 min, MK45 area) or Asda Dunstable (near M1 J11) are cheap starting options
Tebay (J38) is great for lunch but fuel is expensive — if below half, detour to Penrith Tesco J40 (5 min) or Asda Carlisle J44 (20 miles before Gretna, much cheaper)
Leave by 09:00 — M6 busy Saturday afternoons
Plug in BMW on arrival — confirm 3-pin socket with Airbnb host before trip

days.title: Gretna Green → Rowardennan

stops.name: Bothwell Castle, Uddingston
stops.detail: Scotland's finest medieval castle ruin — huge 13th-century Donjon tower on a wooded bluff above the River Clyde. M74 J4, ~2 miles off route. Dogs on lead in grounds. Allow 20–30 min. Historic Environment Scotland site — entry fee; HES members free.

stops.name: Chatelherault Country Park, Hamilton
stops.detail: ~100 miles from Gretna, M74 J6. Stunning Duke's Bridge walk, off-lead woodland. Excellent facilities. 30 min comfort break. Aim ~11:00.

stops.name: Dumbarton Castle — roadside photo stop
stops.detail: Medieval castle on a dramatic 73m volcanic rock at the Clyde/Leven confluence — directly on the A82, zero detour. Dogs not permitted inside but the riverside park at the base is a great 10-min leg-stretch with the castle as backdrop. Mary Queen of Scots left for France from here aged 5.

stops.name: Co-op Food — Balloch (stock-up point)
stops.detail: Pass through Balloch on the way to Rowardennan — best chance to stock up before the remote loch-side road. Carrochan Road, G83 8BW. Open every day 07:00–22:00. Morrisons in Alexandria (~4 miles) for a bigger shop.
stops.maps_query: Co-op Food Carrochan Road Balloch G83 8BW

stops.name: Loch Lomond Shores, Balloch
stops.detail: First sight of the loch. Lochside boardwalk, cafes, dog walk. 30 min stop.

stops.name: Duncryne Hill ('The Dumpling'), Gartocharn
stops.detail: Hidden gem — a tiny round volcanic hill (142m) just off the A811 at Gartocharn giving what many regard as the finest panoramic view of Loch Lomond for the least effort anywhere: islands, Ben Lomond and the Highlands spread out below you. 35–40 min walk up and back, 88m ascent. Dogs welcome on open access land — on lead near livestock. Small layby parking on Duncryne Road (G83 8NE). Free.

stops.name: Drymen Co-op
stops.detail: Last shop before Rowardennan. Stock up for 3 nights. Mon-Sat 07:00-22:00, Sun 08:00-20:00.

stops.name: Balmaha Bay & Jetty
stops.detail: Lochside jetty facing west across to Inchcailloch island — great afternoon light over the water with Ben Lomond behind. 10–15 min stop. National Park car park on site (£2.10/2hrs). Dogs welcome on the shore.

stops.name: Cashel Forest, East Loch Lomond
stops.detail: Millennium native woodland on the lochside, directly on the B837 between Balmaha and Rowardennan — zero detour. Waymarked trails with viewpoint platforms looking across to Ben Lomond. Dogs welcome throughout. Red squirrels, roe deer, osprey on the loch. £3 car park (cash or contactless).

notes: Fuel: Asda Hamilton near Chatelherault (already stopping there) is cheapest on this route — fill up there. Avoid Drymen Co-op petrol — tiny rural forecourt, premium price. No fuel at Rowardennan.
Last 7 miles from Drymen is single-track
Plug in BMW at Airbnb overnight

days.title: Inchcailloch Island + Loch Katrine Cruise

stops.name: SPAR — Drymen (nearest shop, 11 miles)
stops.detail: Rowardennan is remote — SPAR Drymen is the nearest shop (~11 miles south, 14-min drive). 2–4 Stirling Road, Drymen G63 0BN. Mon–Sat 06:30–22:00, Sun 08:00–22:00. Stock up before returning to Rowardennan.

stops.name: Inchcailloch Island Ferry
stops.detail: 5-min ferry from Balmaha. 1.5-mile woodland loop, sandy bays. Dogs ride free. Call ahead day before.

stops.name: SS Sir Walter Scott — Loch Katrine
stops.detail: 45 min drive to Trossachs Pier. Iconic 1899 steamship. Dogs welcome. Book online.

stops.name: Katrine Wheelz e-bikes (optional)
stops.detail: At Trossachs Pier. E-bikes ~35/day. Call 01877 376316 re dog trailer use.

stops.name: Lomond Adventure, Balloch — road & e-bike hire (optional)
stops.detail: Road bikes and e-bikes 14 miles south of Rowardennan. The near-traffic-free B837 lochside lane between Drymen and Rowardennan is ideal. Best as a standalone morning before the day's activities.
stops.maps_query: bike hire Loch Lomond Balloch G83

notes: Call Balmaha Boatyard day before — ferry runs on demand
Book Loch Katrine online for guaranteed slot in May

days.title: WHW Walk + Doune Castle

stops.name: SPAR — Drymen (nearest shop, 11 miles)
stops.detail: Nearest shop to Rowardennan. 2–4 Stirling Road, Drymen G63 0BN. Mon–Sat 06:30–22:00, Sun 08:00–22:00.

stops.name: WHW: Rowardennan to Ross Wood
stops.detail: Flat lochside path — 1.5 miles each way = 3 miles total. STOP at Ross Wood. Do NOT push to Sallochy.

stops.name: Doune Castle
stops.detail: Monty Python Holy Grail filming location. Dogs in grounds. Tag-team the interior.

stops.name: Aberfoyle — e-bike & road bike hire (optional)
stops.detail: Aberfoyle is 10 miles west of Doune on the A873. E-bike hire at the Forest Visitor Centre for Queen Elizabeth Forest Park trails. Road cyclists: the Duke's Pass (A821) is a classic Scottish climb — 5km at 6% with spectacular Trossachs views.
stops.maps_query: bike hire Aberfoyle Queen Elizabeth Forest Park FK8

notes: Max walk today: 3 miles (Ross Wood and back) — well within Koda's limit
Check Doune Castle opening times: historicenvironment.scot

days.title: Rowardennan → Ballachulish + Golf

golf.dogs_note: Dogs confirmed welcome on course — keep on lead near other players

afternoon_tea.name: Inverlochy Castle Hotel
afternoon_tea.detail: 3 miles north of Ballachulish. One of Scotland's finest afternoon teas. Book ahead.

stops.name: Co-op Food — Ballachulish (near stay)
stops.detail: In Ballachulish village, ~1–2 miles from Glencoe village. Park Road, PH49 4JS. Bakery, Costa Coffee, Post Office. Open every day 07:00–22:00. Morrisons Fort William (~10 miles north) for a full supermarket.
stops.maps_query: Co-op Food Park Road Ballachulish PH49 4JS

stops.name: Real Food Cafe, Tyndrum
stops.detail: Best fish & chips on the A82. Dog-friendly outdoor seating.

stops.name: Dragon's Tooth Golf, Woodlands Glencoe
stops.detail: 9 holes, mountain backdrop. Club hire included. Dogs welcome on site.

stops.name: Glencoe Village
stops.detail: Short walk in the valley. NTS Visitor Centre nearby.

stops.name: Nevis Cycles, Fort William — road & e-bike hire (optional)
stops.detail: 8 miles north of Ballachulish on the A82. Road bikes and e-bikes. Glen Nevis road is quieter and scenic — excellent road ride. Good evening option from your Ballachulish base.

eating.name: Real Food Cafe, Tyndrum
eating.detail: Famous for pies, fish & chips and burgers. Dog-friendly outside seating. On the A82 — hard to miss.

notes: A82 north from Tarbet is one of Scotland's great drives — enjoy it
Fuel: Tyndrum and Glencoe garages are expensive rural prices — fill at Stirling Asda or Callander Tesco before heading north on A82 if possible. If caught short, Tyndrum Green Welly has a forecourt.
Plug in BMW at Ballachulish tonight

days.title: Nevis Range Gondola + Jacobite / ScotRail to Mallaig
map_waypoints: Nevis Range Torlundy Fort William PH33 6SW
Fort William Railway Station Fort William PH33 6TQ

stops.name: Morrisons — Fort William (with petrol)
stops.detail: Largest supermarket in the area. An Aird Road, Fort William PH33 6AN. Mon–Sat 07:00–22:00, Sun 08:00–20:00. Petrol station attached. Bakery, butcher, fishmonger.
stops.maps_query: Morrisons Fort William An Aird Road PH33 6AN

stops.name: Nevis Range Gondola
stops.detail: UK's only mountain gondola — 650m up. Dogs ride free, on lead. Snowgoose Cafe at top. Check mountain report night before.
stops.maps_query: Nevis Range Torlundy Fort William PH33 6SW

stops.name: Glenfinnan Viaduct Viewpoint
stops.detail: Drive from Nevis Range to Glenfinnan on the A830 (~20 min). Park at the NTS car park — 10 min walk to the elevated viewpoint for the classic arch-and-loch shot. The Glenfinnan Monument (Jacobite Rising 1745) is right there too, dogs welcome in the grounds. Allow 35 min total. Leave by 11:30 to reach Fort William station for 12:18.

stops.name: Jacobite Steam Train (Option A — if running)
stops.detail: NOT currently bookable — ORR dispute. Sign up for alerts. If open: 2 separate bookings (Koda on one, Monty on other). 10:15 dep, Mallaig 12:25, return 14:10.

stops.name: ScotRail Fort William to Mallaig (Option B — likely option)
stops.detail: Same route, same Glenfinnan Viaduct crossing — diesel not steam. 12:18 dep Fort William, arrive Mallaig ~14:10. Return 16:05 from Mallaig, back Fort William ~18:00. ~1h55 in Mallaig — enough for lunch and harbour walk. Trains are infrequent so do not get off at intermediate stops. Look out both sides ~35 min in for the viaduct — right side sees the arch curve, left side looks down to Loch Shiel.
stops.booking_note: Book off-peak return online at scotrail.co.uk or buy at Fort William station. Off-peak applies after 09:15 on weekdays — 12:18 qualifies. Dogs travel free, no reservation needed for dogs.

stops.name: Mallaig Harbour
stops.detail: Arrive ~14:10. ~1h55 before return train. Walk the harbour, fish & chips at Jaffy's or Cornerstone. Plenty of time.

stops.name: Nevis Cycles / Off Beat Bikes, Fort William — road & e-bike hire (optional)
stops.detail: In Fort William for the train today. Nevis Cycles (road bikes, e-bikes, Lochy Retail Park) and Off Beat Bikes (mountain & e-bikes, High Street) both in town. Good option for a morning ride before the train or after returning from Mallaig.

eating.name: Jaffy's Fish Bar, Mallaig
eating.detail: Arrive Mallaig ~14:10 on the train. Harbour-side fish & chips — eat on the waterfront with the dogs, return train 16:05 so no rush. Ask for a deep fried Mars bar (choc chop) — this is Scotland. Cornerstone Café is a good alternative nearby.

eating.name: Crannog at Garrison West, Fort William
eating.detail: Best seafood in Fort William. Cameron Square. Leave dogs at Ballachulish first. Confirm site is still operating before trip.

notes: Leave Ballachulish by 08:00 for Nevis Range at 08:30
Fuel: Tesco Fort William is the last cheap supermarket petrol before Skye — fill up here today while in town for the train. Kyle of Lochalsh garage (tomorrow) is expensive.
Check gondola mountain report the night before: nevisrange.co.uk/mountain-report
Jacobite: register at westcoastrailways.co.uk for email alerts — book immediately if it opens

ScotRail timings: 12:18 dep Fort William → ~14:10 Mallaig → 16:05 return → ~18:00 Fort William. Book off-peak return online (scotrail.co.uk) or at station — off-peak after 09:15 weekdays, 12:18 qualifies. Dogs free.
Glenfinnan Viaduct viewpoint: after gondola, drive A830 west (~20 min). 35 min stop — walk to viewpoint, see the monument. Leave by 11:30 to reach Fort William station in time.
Do NOT get off the train at intermediate stops — service is infrequent, you risk being stranded for 3+ hours

days.title: Ballachulish → Glendale, Isle of Skye
map_waypoints: Dunvegan Castle Isle of Skye IV55

stops.name: Co-op Food — Kyle of Lochalsh (stock up before Glendale)
stops.detail: Critical stop — stock up here before heading to Glendale (very remote, nearest shop 28+ miles). 1 Bridge Road, Kyle of Lochalsh IV40 8BH. Open every day 07:00–22:00. Right by the Skye Bridge.
stops.maps_query: Co-op Food 1 Bridge Road Kyle of Lochalsh IV40 8BH

stops.name: Eilean Donan Castle
stops.detail: Most photographed castle in Scotland. Dogs in grounds. Tag-team the interior.

stops.name: Kyle of Lochalsh — FUEL
stops.detail: FILL UP HERE. Last reliable fuel before Glendale — 20+ miles with nothing.
stops.maps_query: petrol station Kyle of Lochalsh IV40

stops.name: Skye Bridge
stops.detail: Toll-free. Lay-by on Skye side for photos.

stops.name: Dunvegan Castle (optional)
stops.detail: On route to Glendale. Dogs in grounds and walled garden.
stops.maps_query: Dunvegan Castle Isle of Skye IV55

eating.name: Broadford Co-op (supplies)
eating.detail: Stock up for 3 nights in Glendale.
eating.maps_query: Co-op Broadford Isle of Skye IV49

notes: FILL UP at Kyle — Glendale has NO fuel for 20+ miles. Kyle garage is expensive but unavoidable. Ideally filled cheaply at Tesco Fort William yesterday (Day 6).
Glendale road (B884) is relentlessly single-track — slow down
Sheep everywhere from here — Koda and Monty on leads near livestock
Plug in BMW at Glendale Airbnb — confirm socket with host

days.title: Skye — Trotternish Loop
map_waypoints: Old Man of Storr Car Park Isle of Skye
Kilt Rock Viewpoint Isle of Skye
Quiraing Car Park Isle of Skye
Fairy Glen Uig Isle of Skye

stops.name: Co-op Food — Portree (nearest main shop)
stops.detail: Portree has two Co-ops — Woodpark Road (larger, IV51 9HQ) or Bank Street in the centre (IV51 9DA). Both open every day 07:00–22:00. Only chain supermarket on Skye.
stops.maps_query: Co-op Food Woodpark Road Portree IV51 9HQ

stops.name: Old Man of Storr — LOWER PATH ONLY
stops.detail: Lower car park fills by 09:30. Walk to first viewpoint and back ~1 mile only. SKIP steep upper section.
stops.maps_query: Old Man of Storr Car Park Isle of Skye

stops.name: Kilt Rock & Mealt Falls
stops.detail: 5-min roadside stop. Waterfall into sea. Almost no walking.
stops.maps_query: Kilt Rock viewpoint Isle of Skye

stops.name: Quiraing — first viewpoint only
stops.detail: Car park to first viewpoint and back ~0.6 miles. Skip the full 4.5-mile circuit.

stops.name: Faerie Glen, Uig
stops.detail: 20 min wander, mostly flat. Donation parking.
stops.maps_query: Fairy Glen Uig Isle of Skye

stops.name: Island Cycles, Portree — road & e-bike hire (optional)
stops.detail: In Portree on the Trotternish loop. Road bikes and e-bikes. The Trotternish ridge road (Portree–Staffin–Uig loop, ~40 miles) is Skye's classic road ride. Hire for a few hours while in town for lunch.
stops.maps_query: Island Cycles Portree Isle of Skye IV51

notes: Leave Glendale by 08:30 — Storr car park fills by 09:30, 1hr 50min away
Total walking today: ~2.2 miles — comfortable for Koda
Quiraing single-track road is tight on blind bends
Cloud in? Swap Storr for Brother's Point — nicer in murk and shorter

days.title: Stardust Boat + Sconser Golf + Talisker

golf.course: Isle of Skye Golf Club, Sconser
golf.dogs_note: Dog policy unconfirmed — call 01478 650465 or email iofsgc@gmail.com before trip

map_waypoints: Portree Harbour Isle of Skye IV51
Isle of Skye Golf Club Sconser IV48
Sligachan Hotel Isle of Skye IV47

stops.name: Co-op Food — Portree (passing through)
stops.detail: Co-op on Woodpark Road (IV51 9HQ) or Bank Street town centre (IV51 9DA). Open every day 07:00–22:00. Only chain supermarket on Skye.
stops.maps_query: Co-op Food Bank Street Portree IV51 9DA

stops.name: Stardust Boat Trips, Portree
stops.detail: Wildlife cruise — seals, sea eagles, dolphins. Dogs welcome. 2 hrs. Book ahead.

stops.name: Isle of Skye Golf Club, Sconser
stops.detail: 9 holes with Cuillins AND Red Hills views. Call 01478 650465.
stops.maps_query: Isle of Skye Golf Club Sconser IV48

stops.name: Sligachan Hotel
stops.detail: Iconic mountaineers' hotel. Dog-friendly bar, great ales.
stops.maps_query: Sligachan Hotel Isle of Skye IV47

stops.name: Talisker Distillery, Carbost
stops.detail: THE distillery on this trip. Tag-team — one tours, other walks dogs along Carbost Bay. Book ahead.

stops.name: Island Cycles, Portree — road & e-bike hire (optional)
stops.detail: In Portree for the Stardust boat this morning. Road bikes and e-bikes. Use waiting time before or after the cruise. Portree to Sligachan on the A87 is a quiet scenic road ride (10 miles one way). Tag-team if needed.
stops.maps_query: Island Cycles Portree Isle of Skye IV51

eating.name: Sligachan Hotel
eating.detail: Natural stop between Sconser golf and Talisker. Famous 19th-century coaching inn — huge dog-friendly beer garden with Cuillin views. Pub food all day.
eating.maps_query: Sligachan Hotel Isle of Skye IV47

eating.name: Three Chimneys, Colbost
eating.detail: Iconic Skye fine-dining, 5 min from Glendale. Book 4+ weeks ahead.

eating.name: Portree chip shop — deep fried Mars bar (choc chop)
eating.detail: Any harbour-area chippie in Portree will do a deep fried Mars bar — ask for a 'choc chop'. Classic Scottish guilty pleasure. Eat on the harbour wall while the dogs watch the boats.
eating.maps_query: fish and chip shop Portree Isle of Skye IV51

notes: CALL AHEAD: Sconser golf — confirm dog policy (01478 650465 / iofsgc@gmail.com). If dogs not permitted, Lesley walks dogs along Sconser shoreline or Sligachan while David plays.
BOOK AHEAD: Stardust boat, Talisker tour, Three Chimneys — all sell out in season
Sconser golf: call 01478 650465, club hire available

days.title: Glendale, Skye → Loch Ness (Brackla)

stops.name: Co-op Food — Broadford (stock up before Loch Ness)
stops.detail: Main Street, Broadford IV49 9AE. Open every day 07:00–22:00. Stock up here before Loch Ness — the cottage at Brackla (IV2) is remote with no nearby shops.
stops.maps_query: Co-op Food Main Street Broadford IV49 9AE

stops.name: Broadford Co-op — FUEL
stops.detail: Co-op Broadford is cheaper than Kyle garage — fill up here. Already stopping for supplies. Kyle garage is a fallback if missed.
stops.maps_query: Co-op Broadford Isle of Skye IV49

stops.name: Plodda Falls, Glen Affric
stops.detail: Tomich turn-off on A831. 30-min walk to a 150ft waterfall. Spectacular and quiet.

stops.name: Invermoriston Telford Bridge
stops.detail: Quick roadside stop — old stone bridge over the falls of Moriston.

notes: Falls of Foyers is 5 min from Brackla cottage — save for tomorrow morning
Fuel: If not filled at Broadford, Fort Augustus has a small garage on the A82 (on route). Inverness supermarkets are 30 min off-route but much cheaper if needed.
Brackla is the quiet south shore — away from the tourist crowds
Plug in BMW overnight at Brackla

days.title: Loch Ness Cruise + Urquhart Castle

stops.name: Scotmid Co-op — Drumnadrochit
stops.detail: 1 Carriers Croft, West Lewiston, Drumnadrochit IV63 6AG. Mon–Fri 06:30–22:00, Sat–Sun 07:30–22:00. Hot food to go, fresh and frozen. Also a second Scotmid on Balmacaan Road IV63 6UY.
stops.maps_query: Scotmid Co-op Drumnadrochit 1 Carriers Croft IV63 6AG

stops.name: Falls of Foyers
stops.detail: 5 min from cottage. ~1.5 miles round trip to a 165ft waterfall.

stops.name: Cruise Loch Ness — STANDARD cruise only
stops.detail: Fort Augustus. 1-hour cruise. Dogs ONLY on standard boat, NOT the RIB. Book online.
stops.important: Standard cruise ONLY — RIB does not allow dogs

stops.name: Fort Augustus Canal Locks
stops.detail: Walk the 5-lock staircase with the dogs.

stops.name: Urquhart Castle
stops.detail: Pre-book (15% off). Dogs in grounds — not inside visitor centre. Avoid 11:00-14:00 rush.

stops.name: Loch Ness Hub e-bikes (optional)
stops.detail: Right by Urquhart. E-bikes from ~35/2hrs. Tag-team: one rides, other walks dogs.

stops.name: Bothy Bikes, Drumnadrochit — road & e-bike hire (optional)
stops.detail: 5 min from Urquhart Castle. Road bikes and e-bikes. Drumnadrochit to Invermoriston on General Wade's Military Road (south shore) is quieter and hillier than the A82 — superb road ride with loch views.

notes: Book STANDARD Loch Ness cruise — NOT the RIB. Only standard boat takes dogs.
Loch Ness Experience also worth a visit: lochness.com

days.title: Loch Ness → Edinburgh (via Cairngorms)
map_waypoints: Loch an Eilein Car Park PH22 1QH
House of Bruar Blair Atholl PH18 5TW

stops.name: Tesco Superstore — Aviemore
stops.detail: Best-stocked supermarket in the Cairngorms area. Grampian Road, Aviemore PH22 1RH. Mon–Sat 07:00–22:00, Sun 08:00–20:00. No petrol at this store.
stops.maps_query: Tesco Superstore Grampian Road Aviemore PH22 1RH

stops.name: Loch an Eilein, Cairngorms
stops.detail: 30 min off A9 at Aviemore. Flat 3-mile loop, island castle, off-lead zones. One of the UK's best picnic spots.
stops.maps_query: Loch an Eilein Car Park PH22 1QH

stops.name: House of Bruar, Blair Atholl
stops.detail: The Harrods of the Highlands. Dog-friendly outside. Excellent food hall.
stops.maps_query: House of Bruar Blair Atholl PH18 5TW

stops.name: Edinburgh Zoo (Lesley's option)
stops.detail: If arriving at Portobello by 13:30 — leave dogs at Airbnb, both go together. Open until 18:00, last entry 17:00. No dogs inside.

stops.name: Bothy Bikes, Aviemore — road & e-bike hire (optional)
stops.detail: Right next to the Loch an Eilein / Rothiemurchus turn-off (B970). Road bikes and e-bikes. Could combine the Loch an Eilein walk with a bike loop of the Rothiemurchus Forest. Road cyclists: Strathspey lanes south of Aviemore are excellent — quiet and scenic.

notes: Leave Brackla by 09:30 — need time for Cairngorms + Bruar + Edinburgh
Fuel: Tesco Aviemore (right off the B970 near Cairngorms stop) or Tesco/Asda Perth on the A9 — both significantly cheaper than rural Highland stations. Fill at one of these.
Edinburgh rush hour brutal 16:00-18:30 — use A720 bypass to reach Portobello
Lothian Bus 26: Portobello to Princes Street, 25 min, dogs free
Plug in BMW at Portobello Airbnb

days.title: St Andrews — Town, Coastal Walk & Crail Golf

golf.booking: Walk-in welcome, no advance booking or handicap required. Call ahead in peak season.
golf.dogs_note: Dog policy unconfirmed — call +44(0)1333 450686 or email info@crailgolfingsociety.co.uk before trip

map_waypoints: West Sands Beach St Andrews KY16
World Golf Museum St Andrews KY16 9AB

stops.name: Morrisons — St Andrews (with petrol)
stops.detail: Largest supermarket in St Andrews. 45 Largo Road, KY16 8PJ. Mon–Sat 07:00–22:00, Sun 08:00–21:00. Petrol station on site. Also Tesco Express (138 Market Street) and Sainsbury's Local (71 Market Street) in the town centre.
stops.maps_query: Morrisons St Andrews 45 Largo Road KY16 8PJ

stops.name: West Sands Beach — Chariots of Fire beach
stops.detail: 2 miles of flat sand. Dogs welcome year-round. The Chariots of Fire beach.
stops.maps_query: West Sands Beach St Andrews KY16

stops.name: Swilcan Bridge photo
stops.detail: Walk the public path along the 18th — free and unmissable.

stops.name: St Andrews Cathedral & St Rule's Tower
stops.detail: Atmospheric ruins. Dogs in grounds. Climb tower for the view.

stops.name: R&A World Golf Museum
stops.detail: Opposite 1st tee. Dog-friendly outdoor cafe terrace.
stops.maps_query: World Golf Museum St Andrews KY16 9AB

stops.name: Biketrax, Edinburgh — road & e-bike hire (optional)
stops.detail: 4 miles from Portobello in Tollcross. Road bikes and e-bikes. Best used as a morning ride before driving to St Andrews. Portobello promenade → Leith → Cramond is 20+ miles flat. Pentland Hills 10 miles south for climbs.

eating.name: Cromars fish & chips
eating.detail: St Andrews institution. Ask for a deep fried Mars bar (choc chop) for dessert — they do them. Eat on the harbour or take to West Sands.
eating.maps_query: Cromars Fish and Chips St Andrews

notes: CALL AHEAD: Crail dog policy (+44 1333 450686 / info@crailgolfingsociety.co.uk). If no dogs, Crail harbour and coastal path is 5 min from clubhouse — Lesley walks dogs there.
Old Course requires WHS handicap (max 24M/36W) — not applicable for this trip
Crail Balcomie Links: 10 miles east of St Andrews, cliff-top links, walk-in, no handicap needed
Alternative: Eden or Strathtyrum courses AT St Andrews — no handicap, call 01334 466666
Forth Road Bridge is free — no tolls

days.title: Musselburgh Golf + Edinburgh

golf.dogs_note: Dog policy unconfirmed — call 0131 653 5122 before trip. Note: nearby Royal Musselburgh GC does allow dogs on course.

map_waypoints: Musselburgh Old Links Golf Course EH21

stops.name: Sainsbury's Local — Portobello High Street
stops.detail: 222–228 Portobello High Street, Edinburgh EH15 2AU. Mon–Sat 07:00–23:00, Sun 08:00–22:00. Right on the seafront strip. Tesco also nearby (EH15 1DW).
stops.maps_query: Sainsbury's Local 222 Portobello High Street Edinburgh EH15 2AU

stops.name: Portobello Beach — morning
stops.detail: Right outside the Airbnb. ~1 mile out and back at slow pace for Koda.

stops.name: Calton Hill
stops.detail: Easy 20-min uphill. Best city view. Off-lead at top.

stops.name: Royal Mile — walk DOWNHILL from Castle
stops.detail: Start at Castle Esplanade, walk downhill to Holyrood. ~1 mile gentle slope.

stops.name: Camera Obscura — dogs allowed inside
stops.detail: Well-behaved dogs on leads ARE permitted. Five floors. Good Koda rest stop.

stops.name: Water of Leith: Stockbridge to Dean Village (ONE WAY)
stops.detail: 1.5 miles ONE WAY only — bus back from Dean Village. Do not add the return trip.

stops.name: Biketrax, Edinburgh — road & e-bike hire (optional)
stops.detail: 4 miles from Portobello. Road bikes and e-bikes for hire. Good early morning option before the golf. Water of Leith cycleway, Arthur's Seat loop (6 miles), or Pentland Hills (10 miles south) for a proper climb. Book ahead in June.

eating.name: Blue Lagoon Fish Bar — deep fried Mars bar (choc chop)
eating.detail: Edinburgh institution and the go-to spot for a deep fried Mars bar. Ask for a 'choc chop' — battered and deep fried. Nicholson Street branch is central; South Clerk Street branch is closest to Portobello.

notes: CALL AHEAD: Musselburgh Old Links dog policy (0131 653 5122). If no dogs, Portobello Beach is 10 min away — Lesley walks dogs there while David plays.
Total walking today: ~5 miles — right at Koda's limit. Drop Water of Leith if flagging.
Lothian Bus — tap contactless, dogs free. Bus everywhere between stops.
Don't leave dogs in the car in town at all.

days.title: Edinburgh → York (via Northumberland coast)
map_waypoints: Mercure York Fairfield Manor Shipton Road York YO30

stops.name: Morrisons — Alnwick
stops.detail: Fenkle Street, Alnwick NE66 1JE. Mon–Sat 07:00–22:00, Sun 10:00–16:00. In the town centre — bakery, butcher, 2-hour parking. Also Co-op in Seahouses (~15 miles south, NE68 7RF) if stopping near Bamburgh.

stops.name: Alnwick Castle
stops.detail: Dogs in grounds. Barter Books (enormous second-hand bookshop) is dog-friendly.

stops.name: Bamburgh Castle & Beach
stops.detail: Most dramatic castle in England. Dogs on beach year-round. Miles of sand.

stops.name: Holy Island (Lindisfarne) — TIDE-DEPENDENT
stops.detail: Causeway closes ~5 hrs each tide. CHECK tide times before leaving Edinburgh.

stops.name: Scotch Corner Services — Dog break
stops.detail: ~100 miles from Holy Island. A1(M) J56. Grass area, fuel available. Quick comfort stop before the final 50 miles to York. Aim ~17:00.

eating.name: Ship Inn, Low Newton-by-the-Sea
eating.maps_query: Ship Inn Low Newton by the Sea

notes: CHECK HOLY ISLAND TIDES before leaving Edinburgh: holyislandcrossingtimes.northumberland.gov.uk
If time is tight: Bamburgh beach is more special than Alnwick — prioritise it

Fuel: Asda Hermiston Gait (off city bypass A71 end) or Tesco Corstorphine — both cheap and on the natural route south from Portobello. Fill before hitting the A1 — Northumberland rural stations are expensive.

days.title: York → Home (Pulloxhill)
map_waypoints: Bettys Cafe Tea Rooms York YO1 2QN
Tebay Services southbound M6 J38 Cumbria

stops.name: Tesco Extra — York Clifton Moor (with petrol)
stops.detail: Stirling Road, Clifton Moor Centre, York YO30 4XZ. Mon–Sat 06:00–midnight, Sun 10:00–16:00. ~1–2 miles from Skelton/Shipton Road hotel. Large format — petrol station on site.
stops.maps_query: Tesco Extra Clifton Moor Stirling Road York YO30 4XZ

stops.name: Bettys Cafe Tea Rooms
stops.detail: Leave dogs at hotel — both go together. Book Belmont Room in advance online.
stops.maps_query: Bettys Cafe Tea Rooms York YO1 2QN

stops.name: York City Walls — Bootham Bar to Monk Bar
stops.detail: Best 1-mile section. Dogs on lead. Free.

stops.name: The Shambles & Museum Gardens
stops.detail: Medieval streets + beautiful abbey gardens. Dogs on lead.

stops.name: Tebay Services southbound
stops.detail: The bookend to Day 1. About halfway home. Aim ~14:00.
stops.maps_query: Tebay Services southbound M6 J38 Cumbria

eating.name: Bettys Cafe Tea Rooms
eating.detail: Leave dogs at hotel. Book Belmont Room in advance.

notes: Leave York by 12:00 — M1 Sunday traffic bad from 15:00
Bettys: book Belmont Room in advance online
Welcome home! Trip of a lifetime. Work Monday.

bookings_required.action: Sign up for email alerts at westcoastrailways.co.uk
Enter ballot on TUESDAY 2 JUNE by 14:00

bookings_required.detail: Kitchin / Timberyard / The Witchery — 4-6 weeks ahead
Call Balmaha Boatyard the day before
